text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, driven by Fed uncertainty on interest rates and rising oil prices from Middle East tensions. This is generally negative for your growth-oriented tech and chip names.

Your holdings: SOXL (-31.28%) and SPAL (-27.24%) are leveraged and showing deep drawdowns; margin amplifies these drops significantly. QQQI (-2.11%) is also flagged for a trim and is nearing its stop.

Watch: Keep a close eye on Fed commentary for rate signals and monitor oil prices. Also, watch for any follow-through in chip names after Apple's commitment and Nvidia's strength.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing your leveraged positions and respecting stops in this volatile, rate-sensitive environment.
plays: Re-evaluate SOXL and SPAL. These leveraged ETFs decay and are deep in the red; consider reducing exposure to mitigate further margin risk.
Watch QQQI closely at $55.22. If it breaks below your $52 stop, respect it and trim as indicated by your scanner.
Consider adding to SMH if it confirms the scanner's long setup (entry 580.85-580.27, stop 574.18), supported by positive chip news.
